events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist Brian May was born on July 19, 1947, in Hampton, London.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the iconic Brian May! What's your favourite Queen guitar solo?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones frontman Mick Jagger was born on July 26, 1943, in Dartford, Kent.

angles.name: Still Rolling
angles.copy: 83 years young and still commanding the stage! Happy birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger.

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) officially launched on August 1, 1981, with its very first music video being The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. Did video really kill the radio star? We think not!

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' released
events.blurb: Oasis' groundbreaking debut album 'Definitely Maybe' was actually released on August 29, 1994, becoming a cornerstone of Britpop.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Rewind to 1994! Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe', an album that defined a generation and launched them into superstardom. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Woodstock Festival anniversary
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York, attracting over 400,000 people.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: 57 years since '3 Days of Peace & Music' changed everything. Remembering the iconic Woodstock Festival.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977. He would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Long Live The King
angles.copy: Remembering the one and only King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, on the anniversary of his passing. His legacy lives on.

events.title: Tupac Shakur would have been 55
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur, born on June 16, 1971, was tragically shot on September 7, 1996, in Las Vegas, and died six days later on September 13, 1996.

angles.name: Hip Hop Icon
angles.copy: Remembering the profound impact of Tupac Shakur, who would have been 55 this year. His voice and message continue to resonate.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, widely considered one of the greatest guitarists of all time, passed away on September 18, 1970. He would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Guitar Legend
angles.copy: On the anniversary of his passing, we honour Jimi Hendrix, a true pioneer whose revolutionary guitar playing continues to inspire.

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' released
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road' was released in the UK on September 26, 1969, featuring that legendary zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Iconic Album
angles.copy: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' dropped in '69, giving us one of music's most recognisable album covers. Which side is your favourite?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' frontwoman Chrissie Hynde was born on September 7, 1951, in Akron, Ohio.

angles.name: Rock Rebel
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde, a true rock icon with that unmistakable voice.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis and Beady Eye frontman, and solo artist, Liam Gallagher was born on September 21, 1972, in Burnage, Manchester.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that pure rock 'n' roll swagger.

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' released
events.blurb: John Lennon's timeless anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK in October 1975, though the album came out in 1971.

angles.name: A Song For Peace
angles.copy: 'Imagine' all the people... living life in peace. 51 years since John Lennon gave us this enduring message.

events.title: First iPod released in 2001
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first iPod on October 23, 2001, and it was released on November 10, 2001, revolutionising how we listened to music.

angles.name: Pocket Full of Music
angles.copy: Remember the first iPod? 25 years since Apple changed the game, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket'.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred) to 007, the world's most iconic secret agent.

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles co-founder John Lennon was born on October 9, 1940, in Liverpool.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Remembering John Lennon on what would have been his 86th birthday. His vision for peace and his music live on.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The iconic British comedian, actor, and writer John Cleese was born on October 27, 1939, in Weston-super-Mare.

angles.name: Comedy Legend
angles.copy: Happy 87th birthday to the brilliant John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, a true master of comedy.
